title: Add Ready condition and ServerList status to Memcached CR
summary: Adds a Ready condition to computeConditions() (True when readyReplicas == desiredReplicas && desiredReplicas > 0) and a ServerList []string field to MemcachedStatus (populated with service DNS when Ready=True, nil otherwise). Both API versions (v1alpha1, v1beta1) are updated with the new field, conversion is wired, CRD manifests regenerated, and docs updated. Comprehensive unit tests cover all 8 table-driven scenarios plus SecretNotFound, HPA, and ObservedGeneration paths. Integration tests verify Ready=False/serverList=nil for initial reconciliation and serverList population via fake client. Deepcopy independence tests cover ServerList in both API versions. All blocking and major checks pass.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests exist for Ready condition across all computeConditions scenarios
SecretNotFound tests include Ready=True assertion (Ready independent of Degraded)
HPA tests include Ready assertions for all 4 variants
Integration test: Ready=False and serverList=nil on initial reconciliation
Integration test: serverList populated when all replicas ready (fake client)
Integration test: serverList=nil when not all replicas ready (fake client)
Integration test: zero replicas → Ready=False and serverList=nil
Conversion test: ServerList in fullyPopulated(), explicit DeepEqual assertion
Round-trip test automatically covers ServerList via reflect.DeepEqual
Deepcopy independence test covers ServerList mutation in v1alpha1 and v1beta1
Existing envtest tests updated from HaveLen(3) to HaveLen(4)
Existing HPA test updated with Ready condition presence check
Edge case: zero replicas → Ready=False (not vacuously true)
Edge case: rolling update with all ready → Ready=True

code_quality_checklist.item: Ready condition follows same pattern as Available/Progressing/Degraded
Constants follow existing naming convention (ConditionType*, ConditionReason*)
ServerList field follows existing status field patterns (+optional, json omitempty)
Conversion follows existing field-by-field copy pattern
Ready condition messages are descriptive and context-sensitive
No dead code or commented-out code

security_checklist.item: No user input processed (status is controller-computed)
No secrets or sensitive data in serverList
No external calls or injection vectors

architecture_checklist.item: Ready condition is independent of Available/Progressing/Degraded (additive)
serverList computation is in reconcileStatus, not computeConditions (correct separation)
Both API versions have identical ServerList field definitions
CRD manifest includes serverList in both v1alpha1 and v1beta1 schemas

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic between condition computation and serverList computation
No over-engineering — simple boolean checks and string formatting

fail_fast_checklist.item: Ready condition computed from same inputs as other conditions (no extra fetches)
serverList set to nil when Ready=False (fail-safe default)

defensive_checklist.item: Zero replicas handled (Ready=False, not vacuously true)
readyCond nil check before accessing Status field

issues_found.description: Hardcoded port 11211 in serverList format string instead of using the PortMemcached constant defined in deployment.go:281. All other non-test code (service.go:30, networkpolicy.go:28) references PortMemcached.
issues_found.fix: Change `fmt.Sprintf("%s.%s:11211", mc.Name, mc.Namespace)` to `fmt.Sprintf("%s.%s:%d", mc.Name, mc.Namespace, PortMemcached)`

issues_found.description: Godoc comment on ServerList field says 'addresses of the Memcached pods in host:port format (e.g. "10.244.0.5:11211")' and 'from the ready pod endpoints', but the actual implementation produces service DNS entries (e.g. "my-cache.default:11211"). The CRD reference docs are correct; only the godoc/CRD description is misleading. Same issue in api/v1alpha1/memcached_types.go:327.
issues_found.fix: Update godoc to: 'ServerList contains the Memcached service endpoint in host:port format (e.g. "my-cache.default:11211"). The controller populates this with the headless Service DNS entry when the instance is Ready, nil otherwise.'

suggested_improvements: Use PortMemcached constant in status.go:197 instead of hardcoded 11211 for consistency with service.go and networkpolicy.go
Fix ServerList godoc comment in both API versions to describe service DNS (not pod IPs) to match actual behavior

detected_patterns.name: Status condition computation pattern with capacity-preallocated slice
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding or modifying status conditions in computeConditions()
detected_patterns.description: All status conditions are computed in computeConditions() as a capacity-preallocated slice (make([]metav1.Condition, 0, N)). Each condition follows the same structure: (1) compute boolean from replica/deployment state, (2) set status/reason pair based on boolean, (3) compute human-readable message with context, (4) append metav1.Condition with Type, Status, Reason, Message, LastTransitionTime, and ObservedGeneration. The capacity N must match the number of conditions. Conditions are then applied to the CR via meta.SetStatusCondition in reconcileStatus().

examples.code:
ready := desiredReplicas > 0 && readyReplicas == desiredReplicas
readyStatus, readyReason := metav1.ConditionFalse, ConditionReasonNotReady
readyMsg := "Memcached instance is not ready"
if ready {
	readyStatus, readyReason = metav1.ConditionTrue, ConditionReasonReady
	readyMsg = fmt.Sprintf("All %d replicas are ready", desiredReplicas)
}
conditions = append(conditions, metav1.Condition{
	Type:               ConditionTypeReady,
	Status:             readyStatus,
	Reason:             readyReason,
	Message:            readyMsg,
	LastTransitionTime: now,
	ObservedGeneration: mc.Generation,
})
